fields.description:
## Context / Problem

ADR 0035 §(d) ("Adding a second backend") was only a prose sketch, so the concrete backend
interface for Phase 2 (epic `bbf1-82e1-cf9d-494a`) was unspecified. That blocks decomposition
and plan-review of the implementation stories (S2–S5), which must cite concrete Protocol
names. This story lands the §(d) revision that pins the interface.

Edit-set (docs only): `docs/adr/0035-reconciler-vendor-adapter-seam.md`.

## Approach

Rewrite §(d) to pin four decisions, each grounded in the current coupling:

- **rebar's local ticket is the canonical model** — no separate `CanonicalTicket`. The seam
  speaks local-field vocabulary; adapters translate vendor⇄local via the mappers that already
  exist (`outbound_fields.py`, `inbound_fields.py`/`inbound_translate.py`). No parallel schema
  kept in lock-step, no redundant `rebar⇄canonical` hop.
- **Core owns diff/apply; adapters only read + enact.**
- **Role Protocols behind one `Backend` facade** — `TicketTransport`, `OutboundMapper`,
  `InboundMapper`, `FieldSanitizer`, `IdentityConvention`, plus opt-in capability Protocols
  (`SupportsLinks`, `SupportsComments`, `SupportsIncremental`).
- **One new type `RemoteRef{vendor, instance, remote_id}`** (identity tuple), replacing the
  hardcoded `"jira"` literal in `apply_inbound_records.py` and the bare `jira_key`.

Also update the Status line, Decision points 5–6, and Consequences to reference the pinned
design and scope the first real second backend to epic `be74-7832-03a8-48ac`.

## Scope

- Revised §(d): the role-Protocol table, the `Backend` facade, `RemoteRef`, the registry
  keyed on `config.reconciler.backend`, the three leak-fixes, and the proof-of-seam
  (contract suite + test-only `FakeBackend`; the real GitHub adapter deferred to epic
  `be74`).
- Status/Decision/Consequences edits for consistency.
- No code changes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] §(d) names all five role Protocols, the `Backend` facade, `RemoteRef`, and the registry
  keyed on `config.reconciler.backend` — verified by reading the rendered section.
- [ ] The ADR states the canonical model is rebar's own ticket (no separate model) and lists
  the three leak-fixes (ADF-sizing out of the differ, lossy-status/parent rules into the Jira
  adapter, duplicated value-maps/link-constants single-sourced) — verified by `grep` for those
  terms in the file.
- [ ] The Status line references epic `bbf1`; the proof-of-seam scopes the first real second
  backend to epic `be74` — verified by reading.
- [ ] `make lint` passes on the branch (docs-only change introduces no lint error).
